name: terminal-duck
description: A chat participant that sees the commands you actually ran in the terminal and explains what broke.
author: Darren Jaworski <darrenjaworski@gmail.com>

chatParticipants.name: duck
chatParticipants.description: Ask about the commands you just ran in the terminal.

commands.name: fix
commands.description: Propose a fix for the most recent failed command

commands.name: rerun
commands.description: Suggest the next command to run

commands.name: explain
commands.description: Explain what the most recent command did

commands.title: Terminal Duck: Clear captured shell history

terminalDuck.suggestOnFailure.description: Show a subtle status bar item after a non-zero exit in the integrated terminal, offering to ask @duck about the failure. Auto-hides after 60 seconds.

languageModelTools.name: terminal-duck_getRecentCommands
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Returns the user's most recent integrated-terminal commands with their working directory, exit code, and captured output. Call this when you need to understand what the user just ran to diagnose a failure or answer a question about their local environment.
languageModelTools.userDescription: Lets other chat participants and agents see what you ran in the integrated terminal.

limit.description: Maximum number of recent commands to return. Defaults to 6, capped at 20.

scripts.kitchen-sink: npm run format:check && npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm test && npm run compile && npm run package
